---
title: |
  <center> Statistical Research Skill </center>
  <center> Group 6 </center>
subtitle: School of Mathematics, University of Edinburgh
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

### Model Structure

The Bayesian hierarchical spatio-temporal (BHST) model is specified as:

$$
y_{s,t}
=
\mu
+
u_s
+
\delta(t)
+
\gamma_t
+
\epsilon_{s,t},
$$

where:

- $y_{s,t}$ denotes the observed temperature at spatial location $s$ and time $t$.


**Model Components**

- **Global mean ($\mu$)**  
  Represents the overall average temperature level across all time periods and spatial locations.

- **Smooth temporal trend ($\delta(t)$)**  
  Captures long-term climate evolution over time.  
  A second-order random walk (RW2) prior is used to allow a flexible, smooth nonlinear trend without imposing a strict linear structure.
  
- **Spatial structured effect ($u_s$)**  
  Models spatial dependence between neighbouring grid cells.  
  This component is specified using a Conditional Autoregressive (CAR) or BYM2 prior, allowing geographically nearby locations to exhibit similar temperature patterns.

- **Temporal autocorrelation ($\gamma_t$)**  
  Accounts for short-term persistence between consecutive observations.  
  This component is modelled using an autoregressive process of order one (AR(1)).

- **Observation error ($\epsilon_{s,t}$)**  
  Represents unexplained variability, assumed to follow:

$$
\epsilon_{s,t} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2).
$$


The model decomposes temperature variability into: long-term temporal trend, spatial structure, short-term temporal dependence (?), and residual noise (?).

#### R Code
```{r}
# ============================================================
# INLA Spatio-temporal model for monthly TEMPERATURE (tmp)
# + covariates: elevation + distance-to-coast (static)
#
# INPUTS (stable setup):
#   - ds_usa.nc      : NetCDF containing ONLY tmp (time-varying), subds="tmp"
#   - elev_usa.tif   : 1-layer elevation (will be aligned to tmp grid)
#   - d2c_usa.tif    : 1-layer distance-to-coast (will be aligned to tmp grid)
#
# Key fix:
#   ✅ Merge covariates by (lon,lat) coordinates (not by "cell") to avoid cell-index mismatch.
#
# Train: <=2020
# Test : 2021–2024 held out (NA) for prediction
# Models:
#   M0: fixed effects only (elev + d2c)
#   M1: + RW2 trend + AR1 replicated by area
#   M2: + BYM2 spatial
# ============================================================

library(terra)
library(data.table)
library(spdep)
library(INLA)

# ----------------------------
# 0) INPUT FILES
# ----------------------------
nc_file   <- "ds_usa.nc"
elev_file <- "elev_usa.tif"
d2c_file  <- "d2c_usa.tif"

# ----------------------------
# A) Read rasters
# ----------------------------
r_anom <- rast(nc_file, subds = "anom")
stopifnot(inherits(r_anom, "SpatRaster"))
stopifnot(nlyr(r_anom) >= 2)

ref <- r_anom[[1]]
if (is.na(crs(ref))) stop("anom raster has no CRS; cannot align covariates safely.")

r_elev <- rast(elev_file)
r_d2c  <- rast(d2c_file)

# Align covariates to tmp grid (same CRS + same resolution/extent)
if (!same.crs(r_elev, ref)) r_elev <- project(r_elev, ref)
r_elev <- resample(r_elev, ref, method = "bilinear")
r_elev <- r_elev[[1]]

if (!same.crs(r_d2c, ref)) r_d2c <- project(r_d2c, ref)
r_d2c <- resample(r_d2c, ref, method = "bilinear")
r_d2c <- r_d2c[[1]]

# ----------------------------------------
# A1) Time labels for tmp anomalies layers
# ----------------------------------------
full_start <- as.Date("1961-01-01")
tt_full <- seq(full_start, by="month", length.out = nlyr(r_anom))

keep_layers <- which(tt_full >= as.Date("1961-01-01")) #here to subset the years

r_anom <- r_anom[[keep_layers]]
tt <- tt_full[keep_layers]

names(r_anom) <- format(tt, "%Y-%m")

# ----------------------------
# A2) Build long table (cell x month)
# ----------------------------
df_anom <- as.data.frame(r_anom, xy = TRUE, cells = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE)

dt <- melt(as.data.table(df_anom),
           id.vars = c("cell","x","y"),
           variable.name = "ym", value.name = "anom")

setnames(dt, old = c("x","y"), new = c("lon","lat"))

dt[, year  := as.integer(substr(ym, 1, 4))]
dt[, month := as.integer(substr(ym, 6, 7))]

ym_levels <- sort(unique(dt$ym))
dt[, t_id := match(ym, ym_levels)]

# response
dt[, y_obs := anom]

# ----------------------------
# A3) Domain filter: keep only cells with at least one observed month
# ----------------------------
cell_ok <- dt[, .(keep = any(!is.na(y_obs))), by = cell][keep == TRUE, cell]
dt <- dt[cell %in% cell_ok]

# ----------------------------
# A4) Add covariates (MERGE BY lon/lat)
# ----------------------------
df_elev <- as.data.frame(r_elev, xy = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE)  # x y value
df_d2c  <- as.data.frame(r_d2c,  xy = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE)

# convert to data.table BEFORE using :=
df_elev <- as.data.table(df_elev)
df_d2c  <- as.data.table(df_d2c)

# rename value columns safely (3rd column is value)
setnames(df_elev, names(df_elev)[3], "elev")
setnames(df_d2c,  names(df_d2c)[3],  "dist_to_coast")

# round coords to avoid floating-point join issues
df_elev[, `:=`(x = round(x, 6), y = round(y, 6))]
df_d2c[,  `:=`(x = round(x, 6), y = round(y, 6))]
dt[,      `:=`(lon = round(lon, 6), lat = round(lat, 6))]

# merge covariates then join to dt
covs_xy <- merge(df_elev, df_d2c, by = c("x", "y"), all = TRUE)
dt <- merge(dt, covs_xy, by.x = c("lon","lat"), by.y = c("x","y"), all.x = TRUE)

# drop rows missing covariates (avoid silent drops inside INLA)
dt <- dt[!is.na(elev) & !is.na(dist_to_coast)]

# scale covariates
dt[, elev_sc := as.numeric(scale(elev))]
dt[, d2c_sc  := as.numeric(scale(log1p(dist_to_coast)))]

# ----------------------------
# A5) Re-index spatial id AFTER all filtering
# ----------------------------
stopifnot(nrow(dt) > 0)
dt[, area_id := as.integer(factor(cell))]

# ----------------------------
# B) Train/test split + prediction frame
# ----------------------------
dt[, set := fifelse(year <= 2020, "train", "test")]

dt_truth <- copy(dt)

dt_inla <- copy(dt)
dt_inla[set == "test", y_obs := NA_real_]

S <- max(dt_inla$area_id)

# AR1 index removed to increase computing performance
#dt_inla[, t_ar := t_id]  # AR1 index replicated by area

# ----------------------------
# C) Adjacency graph (rook) for filtered grid
# ----------------------------
cells <- unique(dt_inla[, .(area_id, lon, lat)])
setorder(cells, lat, lon)

ux <- sort(unique(cells$lon))
uy <- sort(unique(cells$lat))
ncol <- length(ux)
nrow <- length(uy)

cells[, col := match(lon, ux)]
cells[, row := match(lat, uy)]

mat <- matrix(NA_integer_, nrow = nrow, ncol = ncol)
mat[cbind(cells$row, cells$col)] <- cells$area_id
idx_to_area <- as.vector(mat)

nb_full <- cell2nb(nrow, ncol, type = "rook")
nb_area <- vector("list", length = S)

for (idx in seq_along(nb_full)) {
  a <- idx_to_area[idx]
  if (is.na(a)) next
  neigh_idx  <- nb_full[[idx]]
  neigh_area <- idx_to_area[neigh_idx]
  neigh_area <- neigh_area[!is.na(neigh_area)]
  nb_area[[a]] <- sort(unique(as.integer(neigh_area)))
}
for (i in seq_len(S)) if (is.null(nb_area[[i]])) nb_area[[i]] <- integer(0)

write_inla_graph <- function(nb, file) {
  S <- length(nb)
  con <- file(file, open = "wt")
  on.exit(close(con), add = TRUE)
  writeLines(as.character(S), con)
  for (i in seq_len(S)) {
    neigh <- nb[[i]]
    if (is.null(neigh)) neigh <- integer(0)
    neigh <- sort(unique(as.integer(neigh)))
    writeLines(paste(c(i, length(neigh), neigh), collapse = " "), con)
  }
}

graph_file <- file.path(tempdir(), "usa_grid.graph")
write_inla_graph(nb_area, graph_file)
g <- inla.read.graph(graph_file)

# ----------------------------
# D) Fit INLA models (Gaussian)
# ----------------------------
ctrl_pred <- list(compute = TRUE, link = 1)
ctrl_comp <- list(dic = TRUE, waic = TRUE, cpo = FALSE)
ctrl_fxd  <- list(
  mean = list("(Intercept)" = 0),
  prec = list("(Intercept)" = 1/100)   # var=100
)

inla.setOption(num.threads = 8) # setting thread to laptop cpu for faster work

# M0: fixed effects only
m0 <- inla(
  y_obs ~ 1 ,
  data = dt_inla, family = "gaussian",
  control.fixed = ctrl_fxd,
  control.predictor = ctrl_pred,
  control.compute = ctrl_comp
)

# M1: + RW2 trend
m1 <- inla(
  y_obs ~ 1 +
    f(t_id, model = "rw2", constr = TRUE),
  data = dt_inla, family = "gaussian",
  control.fixed = ctrl_fxd,
  control.predictor = ctrl_pred,
  control.compute = ctrl_comp
)

# M2: + BYM2 spatial
m2 <- inla(
  y_obs ~ 1 +
    f(t_id, model = "rw2", constr = TRUE) +
    f(area_id, model = "bym2", graph = g, scale.model = TRUE, constr = TRUE),
  data = dt_inla, family = "gaussian",
  control.fixed = ctrl_fxd,
  control.predictor = ctrl_pred,
  control.compute = ctrl_comp
)

# M3: + covariates
m3 <- inla(
  y_obs ~ 1 + elev_sc + d2c_sc +
    f(t_id, model = "rw2", constr = TRUE) +
    f(area_id, model = "bym2", graph = g, scale.model = TRUE, constr = TRUE),
  data = dt_inla, family = "gaussian",
  control.fixed = ctrl_fxd,
  control.predictor = ctrl_pred,
  control.compute = ctrl_comp
)

# ----------------------------
# E) Compare WAIC/DIC + Test RMSE (2021–2024)
# ----------------------------
get_metrics <- function(mod){
  data.table(DIC = mod$dic$dic, WAIC = mod$waic$waic)
}

score_test <- function(mod, dt_inla, dt_truth){
  idx_test <- which(dt_inla$set == "test")
  pred  <- mod$summary.linear.predictor$mean[idx_test]
  truth <- dt_truth$y_obs[idx_test]
  ok <- !is.na(truth) & !is.na(pred)
  pred <- pred[ok]; truth <- truth[ok]
  data.table(
    n_test = length(truth),
    RMSE = sqrt(mean((pred - truth)^2)),
    MAE  = mean(abs(pred - truth))
  )
}

mods <- list(M0 = m0, M1 = m1, M2 = m2, M3 = m3)

metrics <- rbindlist(lapply(names(mods), function(nm){
  cbind(model = nm, get_metrics(mods[[nm]]))
}))
test_scores <- rbindlist(lapply(names(mods), function(nm){
  cbind(model = nm, score_test(mods[[nm]], dt_inla, dt_truth))
}))

print(metrics[order(WAIC)])
print(test_scores[order(RMSE)])

# fixed effects summary
print(m3$summary.fixed)
```
